intent: Propose an exact falsifiability test and channel-separating benchmarks for certificate inventory, depletion, and buyer inspection.

entity.title: Does certificate inventory create a non-monotone inspection return?
entity.summary: A falsifiable proposal linking certification cost to the stationary certificate stock, the uncertified pool, buyer inspection, successful allocation, and buyer surplus.

payload.phenomenon: Lower certification cost can change both the inflow into the stock of visible certificates and its trade-dependent outflow. The resulting stationary mass and capacity composition of uncertified sellers determines what an inspection can reveal and when a buyer chooses to pay c.
payload.object_to_explain: For a fixed admissible capacity law, seller margin and discount factor, search cost c, and one equilibrium-selection rule, let S(k) be the selected stationary distribution of the two sellers' certificate indicators, q_U(k) the probability an uncertified seller is high at the buyer's inspection information set, I(k) the ex ante inspection probability, A(k) the ex ante probability of trading with a high-capacity seller, and B(k)=A(k)-c I(k). Determine whether there exist 0<k_L<k_M<k_H such that, for some Y in {I,A,B}, [Y(k_M)-Y(k_L)][Y(k_H)-Y(k_M)]<0. Then test whether the same strict reversal remains when carryover, trade depletion, direct cost accounting, or adjustment of the inspection rule is removed one at a time under the same selection rule.
payload.unresolved_delta: The unresolved issue is whether a strict reversal can be assigned to the joint law of certificate inventory and uncertified-pool composition rather than to a direct change in seller certification expenditure, a buyer inspection response with no stock, or equilibrium switching.
payload.importance: The distinction determines whether certification-cost policy can be evaluated as a static information-supply intervention or must be analyzed as an inventory intervention that alters the residual population on which costly buyer information acquisition operates.
payload.kill_condition: Kill the inventory-composition mechanism if, for every admissible primitive vector and every cost triple evaluated with one fixed equilibrium-selection rule, no outcome satisfies the strict sign-change inequality; also kill attribution to this mechanism if every candidate reversal is unchanged in the stock-free reset benchmark. Discard any example whose reversal is produced only by selecting different equilibria at different costs.
payload.proposed_scope: The two-seller, infinite-buyer environment in the seed, with truthful certification available only to a high-capacity seller, one visible certificate indicator per seller, certificate carryover until that seller trades, inspection of at most one uncertified seller, stationary symmetric Markov behavior, and one declared equilibrium-selection rule common across costs. The scope excludes solution, directional claims, and welfare ranking.
prohibited_claims: No comparative-static sign or turning point is claimed to exist.
No equilibrium existence, uniqueness, proof, validation, or welfare conclusion is claimed.
No difference across benchmark solutions is attributed to one channel if the primitives or equilibrium-selection rule differ.

entity.title: Certificate inventory channel decomposition
entity.summary: A full model and four controlled variants identify carryover, trade depletion, direct cost accounting, and endogenous inspection adjustment without imposing a result.

benchmarks.label: Full consumable inventory
exact_primitives: Two long-lived sellers and one new short-lived buyer in each period.
A seller privately observes high or low capacity; only a high-capacity seller without a visible certificate may pay k to issue a truthful certificate.
An issued certificate is publicly visible and remains in stock after no trade.
The buyer may inspect one uncertified seller at cost c, buy with or without that inspection, or exit.
Trade with a certified seller removes exactly that seller's certificate before the next period.
The capacity law, seller margin and discount factor, and public equilibrium-selection rule are held fixed across k.
timing: The inherited certificate indicators are publicly observed.
Eligible sellers privately observe capacity and choose certification at cost k.
The buyer observes certificate indicators and chooses whether to inspect one uncertified seller at cost c.
The buyer chooses a seller or exits, service and payoffs occur, and a traded certificate is consumed.
Every untraded certificate carries to the next period.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium with one predeclared selection rule applied at every k; retain the correspondence if that rule does not select uniquely.
benchmarks.prediction: Compute S(k), q_U(k), I(k), A(k), and B(k), and apply the strict three-cost sign-change test without imposing a direction.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: All certification, inventory, composition, depletion, inspection, and direct-cost channels remain jointly active.

benchmarks.label: No carryover: certificates reset before the next buyer
exact_primitives: All full-model primitives and the equilibrium-selection rule are unchanged.
Every certificate expires after the current buyer's decision, including when its seller is not chosen, so the next period begins with zero certificate stock.
Within-period certification and inspection remain endogenous.
timing: Capacity, certification, public observation, inspection, choice, and service follow the full-model order.
All certificate indicators reset to zero after the buyer's decision.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the full model's selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-reset difference is the diagnostic for interperiod inventory and its induced uncertified-pool composition; no sign is prescribed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether the strict reversal survives when contemporaneous certification and endogenous inspection remain but certificate inventory cannot accumulate.

benchmarks.label: Carryover without trade depletion
exact_primitives: All full-model primitives and the equilibrium-selection rule are unchanged.
A visible certificate remains visible after its seller trades as well as after no trade.
Only the post-trade certificate transition differs from the full model.
timing: Inherited stock, capacity, certification, observation, inspection, choice, and service follow the full-model order.
After service, no visible certificate is removed because of trade.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Selected stationary symmetric Markov perfect equilibrium under the full model's selection rule.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-nondepleted difference is the diagnostic for the trade-contingent outflow from certificate inventory; no sign is prescribed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether trade-triggered depletion, conditional on persistence, changes q_U enough to alter the sign-change test.

benchmarks.label: Mechanical certification-cost accounting
exact_primitives: Declare a reference cost k0 before comparison.
Freeze seller certification, buyer inspection and choice, certificate transitions, and the stationary state distribution at the selected full-model policies for k0.
For a counterfactual k, replace only the certification payment in seller payoff accounting.
timing: Generate histories with the frozen reference policies and state law.
Hold all buyer actions, service outcomes, and certificate transitions fixed across counterfactual k.
Enter k rather than k0 only in the seller's certification expenditure.
benchmarks.solution_concept: A fixed-policy accounting counterfactual, not an equilibrium away from k0.
benchmarks.prediction: I, A, and B are invariant by construction in this benchmark, so it records only the mechanical change in seller certification expenditure.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: The difference between this accounting path and a re-equilibrated path contains all behavioral and state-distribution responses.

benchmarks.label: Certificate inventory with a frozen inspection rule
exact_primitives: Use the same predeclared reference cost k0.
Fix the buyer's state-contingent inspect-or-not map to its selected full-model map at k0 for all counterfactual k.
At each k, re-solve seller certification, buyer purchase or exit after the imposed inspection action, certificate depletion, and the stationary state distribution.
All remaining primitives and the equilibrium-selection rule match the full model.
timing: The inherited stock and seller certification evolve at the current k.
The buyer follows the imposed k0 inspect-or-not map, then chooses purchase or exit after any signal.
Trade depletion and the stationary state law re-equilibrate subject to that inspection constraint.
benchmarks.solution_concept: Constrained selected stationary symmetric Markov equilibrium with only the inspection map fixed.
benchmarks.prediction: The full-minus-fixed-inspection difference is the diagnostic for endogenous adjustment of inspection, while the constrained benchmark retains re-equilibrated certificate inventory and depletion; no sign is prescribed.
benchmarks.unresolved_delta: Whether any strict reversal remains when inspection cannot adjust to the k-induced change in q_U.

payload.exact_question_delta: Hold the primitive environment and public selection rule fixed. Compare full versus reset to diagnose certificate carryover, full versus never-depleted to diagnose the trade-triggered inventory outflow, the re-equilibrated full path versus frozen-policy accounting to separate behavioral responses from the mechanical cost payment, and full versus fixed-inspection to diagnose adjustment of buyer information acquisition. A qualifying inventory explanation requires the strict three-cost sign change in a declared outcome and cannot rely on switching equilibrium selections.

outcome.rationale: The candidate provides one strict three-cost question and exact comparisons for carryover, trade depletion, direct cost accounting, and endogenous inspection, while leaving all directions and results unresolved.
